name: Power Products
statement: You are given $n$ positive integers $a_1, \ldots, a_n$, and an integer $k \geq 2$. Count the number of pairs $i, j$ such that $1 \leq i  \lt  j \leq n$, and there exists an integer $x$ such that $a_i \cdot a_j = x^k$.
input:
The first line contains two integers $n$ and $k$ ($2 \leq n \leq 10^5$, $2 \leq k \leq 100$).
The second line contains $n$ integers $a_1, \ldots, a_n$ ($1 \leq a_i \leq 10^5$).
output: Print a single integer — the number of suitable pairs.
